title: Podman Auto-Update Does Not Update Some Containers Due to Expired Certificate
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

Podman auto-update complains some certificates are expired for docker.io even though it can pull updates from other containers that are hosted on docker.io just fine. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install containers with the correct update label

2. Update them with podman auto-update

3. notice the error

**Describe the results you received:**
error auto-updating container "ccd..": image check for "redis" failed: error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid: current time 2021-03-25T02:15:51Z is before 2021-04-25T00:00:00Z


**Describe the results you expected:**
All containers to updated normally

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:57:15 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Checked troubleshooting guide but did not compile podman from source

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical raspberry pi 4 with Fedora IoT 34 Aarch64 (I started having this error after updating to version 34 of Fedora).

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

>  Physical raspberry pi 4 with Fedora IoT 34 Aarch64 (I started having this error after updating to version 34 of Fedora).

The `podman info` output states that you're on Fedora 33, can you clarify?  The certificates on the host are outside of Podman's control. Is your machine on the latest updates?

Hello. Thanks for pointing that out. Sometimes the answer is right in plain sight. The issue was that I triggered the upgrade but on that specific system, rpm-ostree did not actually apply the offline updates. I've tried several times afterwards to no avail. Seems like the problem isn't related to podman but to rpm-ostree. Thanks a lot.
